sn11.12:1.1: At Sāvatthī in Jeta's Grove. There the Blessed One said to the bhikkhus:
sn11.12:1.2: "Bhikkhus, in the past, when Sakka, lord of the devas, was a human being, he was a brahmin youth named Magha; therefore he is called Maghava.
sn11.12:2.1: "Bhikkhus, in the past, when Sakka, lord of the devas, was a human being, he gave gifts in city after city; therefore he is called Purindada, the Urban Giver.
sn11.12:3.1: "Bhikkhus, in the past, when Sakka, lord of the devas, was a human being, he gave gifts considerately; therefore he is called Sakka.
sn11.12:4.1: "Bhikkhus, in the past, when Sakka, lord of the devas, was a human being, he gave a rest house; therefore he is called Vāsavā.
sn11.12:5.1: "Bhikkhus, Sakka, lord of the devas, thinks of a thousand matters in a moment; therefore he is called Sahassakkha, Thousand-eyed.
sn11.12:6.1: "Bhikkhus, Sakka's wife is the asura maiden named Suja; therefore he is called Sujampati, Suja's husband.
sn11.12:7.1: "Bhikkhus, Sakka, lord of the devas, exercises supreme sovereignty and rulership over the Tāvatiṁsa devas; therefore he is called lord of the devas.
sn11.12:8.1: "Bhikkhus, in the past, when Sakka, lord of the devas, was a human being, he adopted and undertook seven vows by the undertaking of which he achieved the status of Sakka … ."
